meta-description: Learn about Silver health plans by reviewing the defintion in the HealthCare.gov Glossary.
title: Silver health plan
excerpt: One of 4 categories of Health Insurance Marketplace® plans (sometimes called “metal levels”). Silver plans fall about in the middle: You pay moderate monthly premiums and moderate costs when you need care. Important: If you qualify for “cost sharing reductions” (or “extra savings”) you can save a lot of money on deductibles, copayments, and coinsurance when you get care — but only if you pick a Silver plan.
content:
One of 4 categories of Health Insurance Marketplace® plans (sometimes called “metal levels”). Silver plans fall about in the middle: You pay moderate monthly premiums and moderate costs when you need care. Important: If you qualify for “cost sharing reductions” (or “extra savings”) you can save a lot of money on deductibles, copayments, and coinsurance when you get care — but only if you pick a Silver plan.

Silver plans are the most common choice of Marketplace shoppers.

Related content

  • Marketplace insurance categories
  • Types of plans and provider networks
  • Save on out-of-pocket costs — only with a Silver plan
